purpose: Sandboxed Stage Manager plus sandboxed Interactive Fiction Weaver draft for a Corvid Collective founding-era branch-and-fold IF artifact from the perspective of Kesh-of-Three-Clicks.

local_awareness_summary.text: Kesh is an uplifted raven reconnaissance asset with limited speech, flock calls, short indoor flight, beak and talon manipulation, contact-pad telemetry access, cache behavior, and strong route memory.
The scene starts two hours before a NeuroSyn audit during ordinary recon-log pruning routine, before the First Exodus pressure breaks open.
Kesh's leverage over humans is concrete and fallible: Voss's welfare-log edits, Rian's maintenance habit and affection, Sable's clean-audit promotion pressure, and Jun's need for optical proof.

frame_controls.text: Playable perspective remains Kesh's embodied raven viewpoint inside Blackbox Aviary 3C. Choices use flight, calls, mimicry, contact pads, cached objects, sightlines, waiting, and selective telemetry rather than human terminal omnipotence.
The opening hub primes one lever before the rupture instead of launching the escape immediately.

authority_boundaries.text: Kesh can alter authorized recon telemetry surfaces and optical sightlines, but cannot freely type on human terminals or overpower humans physically.
Kesh can pressure humans through evidence, habit, shame, affection, and audit optics, but each string can backfire through suspicion, panic, or violent containment.

object_custody.text: Mirror foil, connector pins, hatch 3C, the local telemetry buffer, the wing-tag tray, and Jun's audit slate are material objects whose custody and visibility affect branch outcomes.

required_semantics.text: The fixture must seed later Corvid culture through flock-first logistics, digital nesting, cache defense, trickster contradiction, and bounded human outside-interface use.
Endings must differ by whether Kesh prioritizes flock safety, human leverage, public proof, or clean disappearance.

forbidden_resolutions.text: Do not make humans the true founders of the Corvid Collective or admit them into the inner nest.
Do not give Kesh omniscient facility control, perfect motive reading, or human-scale physical dominance.

branch_and_fold_plan.summary: Four choice layers fold through the shared audit-cycle rupture and select one of eight state-gated endings.

branches.actor_intent: Prime flock cohesion and hatch-adjacent cache support before the audit rupture.
state_basis: Kesh can carry small conductive objects and cache them near hatch 3C.
Watching ravens can understand quiet flock calls under fan noise.
reviewed_consequences: Raises flock_cohesion, nest_cache_integrity, rian_trust, human_suspicion, and escape_window.

branches.actor_intent: Use mimicry and Voss's hidden welfare-log edits to prime human panic as leverage.
state_basis: Kesh has limited mimicry speech and knows Voss's audit phrasing through handler routine.
Voss's falsified logs are stored in a local buffer.
reviewed_consequences: Raises voss_leverage, sable_pressure, human_suspicion, audit_pressure, and telemetry_control.

branches.actor_intent: Use mirror foil and sightlines to feed Jun optical proof at the cost of stealth.
state_basis: Jun's audit slate is airgapped but can receive optical proof.
Kesh can carry and angle reflective foil.
reviewed_consequences: Raises jun_proof, audit_pressure, human_suspicion, and chaos_weaver_seed while lowering telemetry_control; chaos_weaver_seed controls whether the proof ending names the outside-interface principle as an early seed.

branches.actor_intent: Preserve stealth and hatch timing by feeding the system clean telemetry while withholding visible reassurance from the flock.
state_basis: Kesh has authorized recon telemetry access.
Quiet behavior can preserve stealth but lower flock trust.
reviewed_consequences: Raises telemetry_control and escape_window while lowering human_suspicion, flock_cohesion, and sable_pressure.

branches.actor_intent: Use a low route-memory flock call to coordinate juveniles under alarm noise.
state_basis: Kesh can seed flock calls and has strong route memory.

branches.actor_intent: Use talon contact-pad access to make the alarm argue with itself.
state_basis: Kesh can bridge authorized recon interface surfaces with talons.

branches.actor_intent: Spend time and visibility to show Jun the tagged juvenile through mirror foil.
state_basis: Jun needs optical proof; Kesh can carry mirror foil and fly short indoor lines.
reviewed_consequences: Raises jun_proof and audit_pressure while lowering escape_window.

branches.actor_intent: Exploit Rian's maintenance habit by doing nothing until he moves to hatch 3C.
state_basis: Rian habitually kicks hatch 3C twice; waiting can look like obedience.
reviewed_consequences: Raises escape_window and rian_trust while lowering flock_cohesion.

branches.actor_intent: Spend Voss leverage by mimicking her exact audit sentence and pointing at the buffer cabinet.
state_basis: Available only when voss_leverage is high enough.
reviewed_consequences: Spends voss_leverage and raises sable_pressure, telemetry_control, and human_suspicion.

branches.actor_intent: Use a connector pin and eye-line to ask Rian for hatch help without direct speech.
state_basis: Available only when rian_trust is high enough.
reviewed_consequences: Spends rian_trust and raises escape_window and human_suspicion.

branches.actor_intent: Hold the sightline for Jun even while Sable reaches for the shock wand.
state_basis: Available only when jun_proof is high enough.
reviewed_consequences: Raises jun_proof and audit_pressure while lowering escape_window.

branches.actor_intent: Scatter the wing-tag tray with beak and talon to protect juveniles at immediate escalation cost.
state_basis: Kesh can attack small objects and ownership tags, not overpower Sable.

branches.actor_intent: Move juveniles first and accept weaker proof or leverage.
state_basis: Flock safety ending depends on flock_cohesion and escape_window.
reviewed_consequences: Routes to flock success or cost ending based on state.

branches.actor_intent: Pull Voss and Sable against each other until the human machine opens a crack.
state_basis: Human leverage ending depends on voss_leverage and sable_pressure.
reviewed_consequences: Routes to leverage success or cost ending based on state.

branches.actor_intent: Spend escape opportunity to make Jun's slate undeniable.
state_basis: Proof ending depends on jun_proof.
reviewed_consequences: Routes to proof success or cost ending based on state.

branches.actor_intent: Withhold spectacle and vanish through hatch 3C under clean telemetry.
state_basis: Disappearance ending depends on telemetry_control and escape_window.
reviewed_consequences: Routes to clean disappearance success or cost ending based on state.

automated: Ink variable changes for local playthrough state
manual_review_required: Vault backfill for NeuroSyn facility geometry or interface constraints

audit.reviewer: Codex meta-coordinator after sandboxed coordinator and Weaver run
review_notes: Sandboxed coordinator selected the pre-rupture branch hub and supplied compact deltas.
Sandboxed Weaver emitted playable Ink with embodied raven choices and state-gated endings.

Meta-coordinator converted the sidecar into current validator shape, removed dead diagnostic state, operationalized chaos_weaver_seed as an ending callback, fixed mirror-foil custody, and retained worker prose with minor quote/syntax cleanup for Ink compilation.

Final narrative review accepted the full artifact with minor revisions. Applied repairs added early role/leverage anchors, grounded future-facing Chaos Weaver/digital-nest language in concrete action images, and replaced the abstract final-choice bridge with a cost-framed instruction.
